reforms.description: From January 2016, a new R&D tax credit that supports internal R&D investments has replaced the previous tax relief for acquiring new technology. Additionally, the government plans to increase tax breaks for entrepreneurs who reinvest their profits and reduce the corporate income tax rate for SMEs from 19% to 15%, aiming to stimulate innovation and entrepreneurship.
reforms.component_name: R&D Tax Credit and SME Tax Breaks
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The reforms are designed to enhance R&D spending and stimulate innovation among firms, which is expected to raise productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The new R&D tax credit has been in effect since January 2016.
reforms.importance_rationale: The introduction of a new R&D tax credit and significant tax breaks for SMEs represents a structural change in the innovation support system.
reforms.source_quote: From January 2016, a new R&D tax credit that also supports internal R&D investments has replaced the tax relief for acquiring new technology.

reforms.description: The Polish government announced the creation of the Polish Development Fund (PFR) in February 2016, which aims to integrate existing institutions to enhance efficiency in financing infrastructure projects, SMEs, business investment, export promotion, and innovation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The establishment of the PFR is expected to improve the efficiency of financing mechanisms, thereby supporting innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The announcement of the PFR was made in February 2016.
reforms.importance_rationale: The creation of the PFR represents a significant structural change in how innovation and business investments are financed in Poland.
reforms.source_quote: The new government announced the creation of the Polish Development Fund (PFR) in February 2016.

reforms.description: The Ministry of Infrastructure and Development released a draft version of changes to the law on Special Purpose Vehicles in September 2015 to boost road construction based on commercial rules, aiming to enhance the efficiency of infrastructure projects.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The proposed changes aim to improve the efficiency of infrastructure projects, which can indirectly support innovation by enhancing the overall business environment.
reforms.status_evidence: The draft changes were released in September 2015.
reforms.importance_rationale: While the reform is significant, it is more of an adjustment to existing laws rather than a major overhaul.
reforms.source_quote: The Ministry of Infrastructure and Development released a draft version of changes to the law on Special Purpose Vehicles in September 2015 to boost road construction based on commercial rules.
